'''Lee Jackson''' (born 12 March 1969) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s. He was a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], [[England national rugby league team|England]], [[Hull F.C.]] (two spells), the [[Sheffield Eagles]], the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], the [[Newcastle Knights]], the [[Leeds Rhinos]] and the [[York City Knights]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Biography==
Hull-born Jackson was arguably the most talented British hooker of his generation. He featured on the [[1990 Great Britain Lions tour|1990 Lions tour]] and played in [[Hull FC]]'s 14–4 victory over [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in the [[Rugby League Premiership|Premiership]] Final during the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] at [[Old Trafford]], Manchester on 12 May 1991,<ref name="Caught in time - Hull win 1991 RL Premiership">{{cite web|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/more_sport/article1434554.ece|title=Caught in time – Hull win 1991 RL Premiership|work=The Times|date=25 February 2007|accessdate=1 January 2008}}{{dead link|date=September 2024|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> before joining Sheffield Eagles. He was selected to go on the [[1992 Great Britain Lions tour of Australia and New Zealand]].

Lee Jackson scored the fastest ever try in either code of rugby, after 9-seconds for [[Hull F.C.]] in the 8–12 defeat by Sheffield Eagles in the [[1992 Yorkshire Cup]] semi-final during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] at the [[Don Valley Stadium]] on Tuesday 6 October 1992.<ref name="Guinness World Record">{{cite web|url=http://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/content_pages/record.asp?recordid=44608 |title=Guinness World Record |publisher=guinnessworldrecords.com |date=31 December 2011 |accessdate=1 January 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20060317032314/http://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/content_pages/record.asp?recordid=44608 |archivedate=17 March 2006 }}</ref>

In 1993, Sheffield Eagles paid [[Hull F.C.]] a fee of £83,000 for Lee Jackson (based on increases in average earnings, this would be approximately £164,900 in 2013),<ref name="Measuring orth - Relative Value of UK Pounds">{{cite web|url=http://www.measuringworth.com/ukcompare|title=Measuring Worth – Relative Value of UK Pounds|publisher=Measuring Worth|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015}}</ref> this makes him the most expensive hooker in the history of rugby league.<ref name="Tickle follows Lydon's lead">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/bbc_pundits/2055091.stm|title=Tickle follows Lydon's lead|publisher=BBC|date=20 June 2002|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>

Jackson was selected to play for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995 World Cup]] Final at {{rlp|hk}} but [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] won the match and retained the Cup.

He later joined the [[Newcastle Knights]], helping them to Grand Final success in 1997. He returned to Britain and had spells with [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Hull F.C.]] He finished his career with [[York City Knights]], appearing 74 times before retiring in 2005.<ref>{{cite web|title=Appearances/Scorers – Season 2003|url=http://www.yorkcityknights.co.uk/mainFrame/appearances03.htm|publisher=York City Knights|accessdate=19 May 2015|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20060928194616/http://www.yorkcityknights.co.uk/mainFrame/appearances03.htm|archivedate=28 September 2006}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Appearances/Scorers – Season 2004|url=http://www.yorkcityknights.co.uk/mainFrame/appearances04.htm|publisher=York City Knights|accessdate=19 May 2015|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20060928194652/http://www.yorkcityknights.co.uk/mainFrame/appearances04.htm|archivedate=28 September 2006}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Appearances/Scorers – Season 2005|url=http://www.yorkcityknights.co.uk/mainFrame/appearances05.htm|publisher=York City Knights|accessdate=19 May 2015|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20060928194555/http://www.yorkcityknights.co.uk/mainFrame/appearances05.htm|archivedate=28 September 2006}}</ref>
